etymology.description: 天(天) (tiān, "heaven; sky") refers to the celestial sphere above, encompassing the sky and the divine forces that influence life; 氣(氣) (qì, "air; atmosphere") represents the vital breath or life force that permeates the world.
etymology.interpretation: 天(天) (tiān, "heaven; sky") serves as the expansive realm above us, a domain of divine powers that influences earthly life. 氣(氣) (qì, "air; atmosphere") captures the concept of an energy or force that flows, suggesting movement and change like the weather. Combining 天(天) and 氣(氣), 天氣 (tiānqì) refers to the conditions of the sky and air, what we call the 'weather'. This includes aspects like sunshine, rain, and wind all stemming from the movement and changes in the sky and air.

examples.chinese: 今天的天气很好(今天的天氣很好)。
天气变得越来越冷(天氣變得越來越冷)。
examples.english: The weather is getting colder and colder.

examples.chinese: 山区的天气变化很快(山區的天氣變化很快)。
examples.pinyin: Shānqū de tiānqì biànhuà hěn kuài.
examples.english: The weather changes very quickly in the mountainous area.
